name: Sam Most
description: American jazz flautist, clarinetist, and tenor saxophonist (1930–2013)
abstract: Samuel Most (December 16, 1930 – June 13, 2013) was an American jazz flutist, clarinetist and tenor saxophonist, based in Los Angeles. He was "probably the first great jazz flutist", according to jazz historian Leonard Feather.

interviews.title: Sam Most interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Wess
about_quotes.evidence: whenever we ran into each other we used to play duets together
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Wess interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 29, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Militello
about_quotes.evidence: when I would go to school Sam basically said okay we’ve got History of Music first period... he would let me stay in the back of the music room and just sleep on a desk.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Militello interviewed by Monk Rowe, Buffalo, New York, October 14, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Red Norvo
about_quotes.evidence: Like the last one I had, oh it was -- it was Sam Most, and Gene Cherico... who else did I have on drums? Oh, I had J. C. Heard.

about_quotes.speaker: Holly Hofmann
about_quotes.evidence: And shortly after that, Sam Most, Joe Farrell, Frank Wess. I’m not quite sure of the chronology there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Holly Hofmann interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 13, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Gene Bertoncini
about_quotes.evidence: Sam was playing a few of the lines. So I went to the rehearsal... I played all these lines.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gene Bertoncini interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: And this one person happened to be Sam Most, and he had this girl drummer with him, too.
